title: Paying yourself first
description: Practice learning the principles behind paying yourself first.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does "paying yourself first" mean in personal finance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: saving a portion of your income before spending on anything else
spending on your needs and wants before paying your bills
borrowing money from yourself before using credit cards

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a good way to start paying yourself first?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Transfer money into your savings account the same day you get paid.
Wait until all your wants and needs are paid for, and transfer the rest of the money into savings.
Asking your boss to pay you on the first of every month.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of paying yourself first?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: putting $\$25$ of your earnings into a savings account every time you get paid
spending all your money on clothes, or movies and saving the change
lending money to your friends or siblings and charging them interest

assessment_items.question_content:
You are currently getting paid monthly. You want to start paying yourself first and have opened a savings account. You want your savings transfers to happen the same day as your paycheck.  

**Which option should you select for transfers?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: once per month, on the same day as your paycheck

assessment_items.question_content:
You are currently getting paid bi-weekly. You want to start paying yourself first and have opened a savings account. You want your savings transfers to happen every time you get paid.

**Which transfer option best matches your pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: once per month, starting on the day of the paycheck

assessment_items.question_content:
You are currently getting paid every 1st and 15th of the month. You want to start paying yourself first and have opened a savings account. You want your savings transfers to happen every time you get paid.

**Which transfer option best matches your pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: once per month, starting on the day of the paycheck

assessment_items.question_content:
You are paid weekly.
 
**How many times do you get paid per year?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times
hints: To answer this question, think about how many weeks are in a year.
There are $52$ weeks in a year.
You are getting paid $52$ times per year.

answers.message: There are $52$ weeks in a year.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are paid biweekly.
 
**How many times do you get paid per year?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times
hints: There are $52$ weeks in a year. Biweekly means every *other* week.
If you are paid biweekly, then you are getting paid $26$ times per year.

answers.message: There are $52$ weeks in a year. Bi-weekly means every other week, so $52 \div 2 = 26$

assessment_items.question_content:
You are paid semi-monthly.
 
**How many times do you get paid per year?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times
hints: There are $12$ months in a year. Semi-monthly means every half month, or *twice* per month.
You are paid $24$ times per year.

answers.message: There are $12$ months in a year. Semi-monthly means every half month, or twice per month. So $12 \times 2 = 24$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Alex has one year to save up for a new TV. The new TV costs $\$884$. He wants to save the minimum amount out of each of his weekly paychecks.

**If Alex saves the same amount each paycheck, how much does he need to save in order to meet his goal?**

$\$$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Alex gets paid weekly, or $52$ times per year.
If Alex wants to save the same, minimum amount, we can divide the cost of the TV by the number of times Alex gets paid  per year.
Alex needs to save $\$17$ per paycheck.

answers.message: Getting paid weekly means 52 times per year. $\$884 \div 52 = \$17$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tara has one year to save $\$1{,}800$ for a family trip. She wants to save the minimum amount from each of her semi-monthly paychecks. 

**If Tara saves the same amount each paycheck, how much does she need to save in order to meet her goal?**

$\$$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Tara gets paid semi-monthly, or $24$ times per year.
If Tara wants to save the same, minimum amount, we can divide the cost of the family trip by the number of times Tara gets paid  per year.
Tara needs to save $\$75$ per paycheck.

answers.message: Getting paid semi-monthly means 24 times per year. $\$1{,}800 \div 24 = \$75$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Andrea wants to build an emergency fund of $\$2{,}340$  over the next year. She wants to save the minimum amount from each of her bi-weekly paychecks. 

**If Andrea saves the same amount each paycheck, how much does she need to save in order to meet her goal?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Andrea gets paid bi-weekly, or $26$ times per year.
If Andrea wants to save the same, minimum amount, we can divide the emergency fund goal by the number of times Andrea gets paid per year.
Andrea needs to save $\$90$ per paycheck.

answers.message: Getting paid bi-weekly means 26 times per year. $\$2{,}340 \div 26 = \$90$.
